content.subject: P9-1: schema + migration + seed for product_lines and product_line_id
content.description: Drizzle schema additions (product_lines table; product_line_id on approval_reviewers, approval_requests, extensions; CHECK constraints; partial unique indexes). Generate migration 0009; add inline seed for the 4 product lines. Update db seed script if needed.

content.subject: P9-2: repo layer — thread productLineId through reviewers + approvals + new productLines repo
content.description: server/repositories/reviewers.ts (listMatrix, listCellsForUser, findReviewersFor, insertReviewer, isCompanyAdminForSubCat). server/repositories/approvals.ts (ApprovalRequestRow, listPendingForCells, isReviewerForCell, setExtensionOfficialTier). New server/repositories/productLines.ts.

content.subject: P9-3: orchestrator — productLine-aware submit/decide + new error codes
content.description: server/utils/approvals.ts SubmitParams gains productLineId; iff-rule; decideRequest passes productLineId; new error codes missing_product_line and not_company_admin.

content.subject: P9-4: validators — require productLineId iff tier=productLine
content.description: SubmitApprovalSchema, AssignReviewerSchema, filtersSchema (shared/validators/).

content.subject: P9-5: API — requireCellAdmin + /api/internal/product-lines + relaxed reviewers GET
content.description: New requireCellAdmin helper. assign/unassign use it. reviewers/index.get returns canEdit, viewer, productLines. submit honours productLineId. /api/v1 contract: confirm unchanged, add explanatory comment.

content.subject: P9-6: admin UI — split ReviewerMatrix into Company + ProductLine subviews
content.description: ReviewerMatrixCompany.vue, ReviewerMatrixProductLine.vue, tabbed shell, canEdit greying, URL hash persistence.

content.subject: P9-7: publish UI — productLine picker in RequestOfficialDialog + queue badge
content.description: RequestOfficialDialog.vue gains productLine select when tier=productLine. ReviewerQueueTable.vue shows third badge.

content.subject: P9-8: listing UI — inline ProductLinePill on filter rail + ExtHero label
content.description: New ProductLinePill.vue, FilterBar plumbing, search/query.ts predicate, ExtHero.vue renders "Wireless Official" style label.

content.subject: P9-9: tests — repository, orchestrator, component, e2e
content.description: Extend reviewers + approvals integration tests; new orchestrator tests; new ReviewerMatrixProductLine unit; extend approval.spec e2e (round-trip + delegation + negative).

content.subject: P9-10: docs — ADR addendum, CLAUDE.md #12, docs/plan.md, features.md
content.description: ADR-0001 2026-06-08 addendum; CLAUDE.md decision #12 rewrite; docs/plan.md §3 + §13; docs/features.md one-liner.

2	// (tier, subCat) cell to one or more reviewer emails. The seed script
3	// resolves emails to user ids against the rows it just inserted, so the
4	// emails here must match `CREATORS` in `scripts/seed.ts`. Real prod
5	// assignment happens through the super-admin matrix UI.
6	//
7	// Spread the assignments so every cell has at least one reviewer and the
8	// reviewer-queue page has non-empty content under each demo persona.
9

11	  tier: "productLine" | "company"
12	  subCat: string
13	  reviewerEmail: string
14	}> = [
15	  // Product-Line tier: per-line lieutenants.
16	  { tier: "productLine", subCat: "systemDesign", reviewerEmail: "amy@agentcenter.dev" },
17	  { tier: "productLine", subCat: "softDev",      reviewerEmail: "ben@agentcenter.dev" },

28	  { tier: "company", subCat: "softDev",      reviewerEmail: "amy@agentcenter.dev" },

content.content:
1	// Dev-seed approval requests for the approval workflow. Covers every
2	// state the publisher and reviewer UIs render, so a freshly-seeded dev
3	// DB lights up `/admin/approvals`, the publisher dashboard's "Awaiting
4	// review" pill, and `/profile?section=requests` without any manual
5	// submit/decide cycle. Mirrors `approval-reviewers.ts`'s shape: emails
6	// and slugs are resolved by `scripts/seed.ts` against the rows it just
7	// inserted, with warnings for any reference that doesn't match.
8	//
9	// Invariant: at most one pending row per extension. The orchestrator
10	// guards this at runtime; the seed must not break it on first load.
11	
12	export interface SeedApprovalRequest {
13	  extensionSlug: string
14	  publisherEmail: string
15	  requestedTier: "productLine" | "company"
16	  subCat: string
17	  status: "pending" | "approved" | "rejected"
18	  reason?: string
19	  decidedByEmail?: string
20	  reviewerNote?: string
21	}
22	
23	export const APPROVAL_REQUESTS: ReadonlyArray<SeedApprovalRequest> = [
24	  {
25	    extensionSlug: "calendar-agent",
26	    publisherEmail: "cory@agentcenter.dev",
27	    requestedTier: "company",
28	    subCat: "data",
29	    status: "pending",
30	    reason:
31	      "Calendar coordination has become load-bearing across the org — promoting this would let teams trust it without a second look.",
32	  },
33	  {
34	    extensionSlug: "mqtt-bridge",
35	    publisherEmail: "dao@agentcenter.dev",
36	    requestedTier: "productLine",
37	    subCat: "network",
38	    status: "pending",
39	    reason: "Already used by every embedded squad in the IoT product line.",
40	  },
41	  {
42	    extensionSlug: "summarize",
43	    publisherEmail: "eli@agentcenter.dev",
44	    requestedTier: "company",
45	    subCat: "docs",
46	    status: "approved",
47	    decidedByEmail: "fei@agentcenter.dev",
48	  },
49	  {
50	    extensionSlug: "explain",
51	    publisherEmail: "fei@agentcenter.dev",
52	    requestedTier: "company",
53	    subCat: "softDev",
54	    status: "rejected",
55	    decidedByEmail: "amy@agentcenter.dev",
56	    reviewerNote:
57	      "Needs a maintainer contact in the manifest before company-level review.",
58	  },
59	]
60

290	  const userIdByEmail = new Map(CREATORS.map((u) => [u.email, u.id]))
291	  const extIdBySlug = new Map(extRows.map((r) => [r.slug, r.id]))
292	
293	  // Approval requests — a small spread covering every UI state. Pending
294	  // rows light up the reviewer queue; the approved row also stamps the
295	  // parent extension's officialTier so the publisher's view matches what
296	  // the real workflow's transactional approve would have produced. NOTE:
297	  // these rows are inserted directly, bypassing `submitRequest`, so no
298	  // `extension/approval.requested` Inngest event fires for them — that's
299	  // expected on a fresh seed and not a bug in the workflow.
300	  const approvalRequestRows: {
301	    id: string
302	    extensionId: string
303	    requestedTier: "productLine" | "company"
304	    subCat: string
305	    requestedByUserId: string
306	    reason: string | null
307	    status: "pending" | "approved" | "rejected"
308	    decidedByUserId: string | null
309	    decidedAt: Date | null
310	    reviewerNote: string | null
311	  }[] = []
312	  const officialTierStamps: { extensionId: string; tier: "productLine" | "company" }[] = []
313	  for (const [i, r] of APPROVAL_REQUESTS.entries()) {
314	    const extensionId = extIdBySlug.get(r.extensionSlug)
315	    if (!extensionId) {
316	      console.warn(
317	        `seed: approval request #${i} references unknown extension slug "${r.extensionSlug}" — skipping`,
318	      )
319	      continue
320	    }
321	    const publisherId = userIdByEmail.get(r.publisherEmail)
322	    if (!publisherId) {
323	      console.warn(
324	        `seed: approval request #${i} references unknown publisher ${r.publisherEmail} — skipping`,
325	      )
326	      continue
327	    }
328	    let decidedByUserId: string | null = null
329	    if (r.decidedByEmail) {
330	      decidedByUserId = userIdByEmail.get(r.decidedByEmail) ?? null
331	      if (!decidedByUserId) {
332	        console.warn(
333	          `seed: approval request #${i} references unknown decider ${r.decidedByEmail} — leaving decidedByUserId null`,
334	        )
335	      }
336	    }
337	    const isDecided = r.status === "approved" || r.status === "rejected"
338	    // Defensive: the orchestrator enforces at-most-one-pending-per-extension
339	    // at runtime; the seed must boot the marketplace in a state that already
340	    // honors it. The current data file picks distinct extensions for the two
341	    // pending rows, but a future edit could silently violate the invariant
342	    // without this guard — skip with a warning instead.
343	    if (
344	      r.status === "pending" &&
345	      approvalRequestRows.some(
346	        (existing) =>
347	          existing.extensionId === extensionId && existing.status === "pending",
348	      )
349	    ) {
350	      console.warn(
351	        `seed: approval request #${i} would be a second pending row for "${r.extensionSlug}" — skipping to preserve the at-most-one-pending invariant`,
352	      )
353	      continue
354	    }
355	    approvalRequestRows.push({
356	      id: `appr-req-${i}`,
357	      extensionId,
358	      requestedTier: r.requestedTier,
359	      subCat: r.subCat,
360	      requestedByUserId: publisherId,
361	      reason: r.reason ?? null,
362	      status: r.status,
363	      decidedByUserId,
364	      decidedAt: isDecided ? new Date() : null,
365	      reviewerNote: r.reviewerNote ?? null,
366	    })
367	    if (r.status === "approved") {
368	      officialTierStamps.push({ extensionId, tier: r.requestedTier })
369	    }
370	  }
371	  if (approvalRequestRows.length > 0) {
372	    console.log(`seed: inserting ${approvalRequestRows.length} approval requests`)
373	    await db.insert(approvalRequests).values(approvalRequestRows)
374	  }
375	  for (const stamp of officialTierStamps) {
376	    await db
377	      .update(extensions)
378	      .set({ officialTier: stamp.tier })
379	      .where(sql`${extensions.id} = ${stamp.extensionId}`)
380	  }
381	  if (officialTierStamps.length > 0) {
382	    console.log(
383	      `seed: stamped officialTier on ${officialTierStamps.length} extensions to match approved requests`,
384	    )
385	  }
386	
387	  // Wipe and re-seed editorial public collections so the /collections browse
388	  // page has realistic material in dev/staging. CASCADE clears
389	  // collection_items as well. Anything a real signed-in user created

content.text:
I have enough context. Now I'll update the schema. Strategy:

- Add `productLines` table to `extension.ts` (mirrors `tags`).
- Add `productLineId` to `extensions`, `approval_reviewers`, `approval_requests` with CHECK constraints.
- Replace the existing unique on `approval_reviewers` with two partial unique indexes.
- Extend the queue index `idx_approval_status_cell` to include `productLineId`.

content.description: server/repositories/reviewers.ts (listMatrix, listCellsForUser, findReviewersFor, insertReviewer, isCompanyAdminForSubCat). server/repositories/approvals.ts (ApprovalRequestRow, listPendingForCells, isReviewerForCell, setExtensionOfficialTier). New server/repositories/productLines.ts.

content.subject: P9-3: orchestrator — productLine-aware submit/decide + new error codes
content.description: server/utils/approvals.ts SubmitParams gains productLineId; iff-rule; decideRequest passes productLineId; new error codes missing_product_line and not_company_admin.

content.subject: P9-4: validators — require productLineId iff tier=productLine
content.description: SubmitApprovalSchema, AssignReviewerSchema, filtersSchema (shared/validators/).

content.subject: P9-5: API — requireCellAdmin + /api/internal/product-lines + relaxed reviewers GET
content.description: New requireCellAdmin helper. assign/unassign use it. reviewers/index.get returns canEdit, viewer, productLines. submit honours productLineId. /api/v1 contract: confirm unchanged, add explanatory comment.

content.subject: P9-6: admin UI — split ReviewerMatrix into Company + ProductLine subviews
content.description: ReviewerMatrixCompany.vue, ReviewerMatrixProductLine.vue, tabbed shell, canEdit greying, URL hash persistence.

content.subject: P9-7: publish UI — productLine picker in RequestOfficialDialog + queue badge
content.description: RequestOfficialDialog.vue gains productLine select when tier=productLine. ReviewerQueueTable.vue shows third badge.

content.subject: P9-8: listing UI — inline ProductLinePill on filter rail + ExtHero label
content.description: New ProductLinePill.vue, FilterBar plumbing, search/query.ts predicate, ExtHero.vue renders "Wireless Official" style label.

content.subject: P9-9: tests — repository, orchestrator, component, e2e
content.description: Extend reviewers + approvals integration tests; new orchestrator tests; new ReviewerMatrixProductLine unit; extend approval.spec e2e (round-trip + delegation + negative).

content.subject: P9-10: docs — ADR addendum, CLAUDE.md #12, docs/plan.md, features.md

+// is a content migration rather than a release.

+    // approval orchestrator is the only writer.

lines: // FUNC_TAXONOMY in shared/taxonomy.ts; validated against the flattened l1
// key set at the validator layer.
+    // Product line snapshot — required iff requestedTier='productLine'.
+    // RESTRICT on delete keeps the audit trail honest: a product line cannot
+    // disappear while requests still reference it.
// Reviewer queue: pending rows in a given cell, ordered by createdAt.
+    // productLineId trails so company-tier rows (productLineId IS NULL) still
+    // collapse onto the same prefix; productLine-tier scans use the full key.
// Publisher "my requests" view and the at-most-one-pending guard.
+    // Shape invariant: productLineId is present iff requestedTier='productLine'.
+      sql`(requested_tier = 'productLine' AND product_line_id IS NOT NULL)
// Same FUNC_TAXONOMY l1 key as approvalRequests.subCat — text rather
// than enum so adding a taxonomy leaf doesn't require a migration.
+    // Product line this reviewer is scoped to. Required iff tier='productLine',
+    // null when tier='company'. CHECK enforces the shape; the unique behaviour
+    // for each tier is expressed below as two partial indexes.
+    // Tier-aware uniqueness. ProductLine cells include productLineId in the
+    // key; company cells do not. Two partial indexes are clearer than relying
+    // on NULLS NOT DISTINCT and let each tier's arity stand on its own.
+    // Shape invariant: productLineId is present iff tier='productLine'.
+      sql`(tier = 'productLine' AND product_line_id IS NOT NULL)

-- Backfill existing rows before the CHECK constraints land. Pre-0009 rows

 WHERE "requested_tier" = 'productLine' AND "product_line_id" IS NULL;
--> statement-breakpoint

-- so coverage is preserved without picking favourites. The new partial unique
-- index allows (tier, sub_cat, product_line_id, user_id) per cell.
INSERT INTO "approval_reviewers" ("id", "tier", "sub_cat", "product_line_id", "user_id", "created_at")
SELECT r."id" || '-' || pl."id", r."tier", r."sub_cat", pl."id", r."user_id", r."created_at"
  FROM "approval_reviewers" r
 CROSS JOIN "product_lines" pl
 WHERE r."tier" = 'productLine' AND r."product_line_id" IS NULL;

CREATE INDEX "idx_approval_status_cell" ON "approval_requests" USING btree ("status","sub_cat","requested_tier","product_line_id");--> statement-breakpoint
CREATE INDEX "idx_approval_reviewers_cell" ON "approval_reviewers" USING btree ("tier","sub_cat","product_line_id");--> statement-breakpoint
ALTER TABLE "extensions" ADD CONSTRAINT "extensions_pl_shape_chk" CHECK (official_tier IS NULL
        OR (official_tier = 'productLine' AND product_line_id IS NOT NULL)
        OR (official_tier = 'company' AND product_line_id IS NULL));--> statement-breakpoint
ALTER TABLE "approval_requests" ADD CONSTRAINT "approval_requests_pl_shape_chk" CHECK ((requested_tier = 'productLine' AND product_line_id IS NOT NULL)
        OR (requested_tier = 'company' AND product_line_id IS NULL));--> statement-breakpoint
ALTER TABLE "approval_reviewers" ADD CONSTRAINT "approval_reviewers_pl_shape_chk" CHECK ((tier = 'productLine' AND product_line_id IS NOT NULL)
        OR (tier = 'company' AND product_line_id IS NULL));

+// it must be omitted (the DB CHECK enforces both). The seed script
// resolves emails to user ids against the rows it just inserted, so the
// emails here must match `CREATORS` in `scripts/seed.ts`. Real prod
-// assignment happens through the super-admin matrix UI.

// Spread the assignments so every cell has at least one reviewer and the

+  // Product-Line tier: per-(subCat × productLine) lieutenants. We fan each
+  // subCat's reviewer across all four product lines so every cell of the

content.description: Drizzle schema additions (product_lines table; product_line_id on approval_reviewers, approval_requests, extensions; CHECK constraints; partial unique indexes). Generate migration 0009; add inline seed for the 4 product lines. Update db seed script if needed.

content.subject: P9-2: repo layer — thread productLineId through reviewers + approvals + new productLines repo
content.description: server/repositories/reviewers.ts (listMatrix, listCellsForUser, findReviewersFor, insertReviewer, isCompanyAdminForSubCat). server/repositories/approvals.ts (ApprovalRequestRow, listPendingForCells, isReviewerForCell, setExtensionOfficialTier). New server/repositories/productLines.ts.

content.subject: P9-3: orchestrator — productLine-aware submit/decide + new error codes
content.description: server/utils/approvals.ts SubmitParams gains productLineId; iff-rule; decideRequest passes productLineId; new error codes missing_product_line and not_company_admin.

content.subject: P9-4: validators — require productLineId iff tier=productLine
content.description: SubmitApprovalSchema, AssignReviewerSchema, filtersSchema (shared/validators/).

content.subject: P9-5: API — requireCellAdmin + /api/internal/product-lines + relaxed reviewers GET
content.description: New requireCellAdmin helper. assign/unassign use it. reviewers/index.get returns canEdit, viewer, productLines. submit honours productLineId. /api/v1 contract: confirm unchanged, add explanatory comment.

content.subject: P9-6: admin UI — split ReviewerMatrix into Company + ProductLine subviews
content.description: ReviewerMatrixCompany.vue, ReviewerMatrixProductLine.vue, tabbed shell, canEdit greying, URL hash persistence.

content.subject: P9-7: publish UI — productLine picker in RequestOfficialDialog + queue badge
content.description: RequestOfficialDialog.vue gains productLine select when tier=productLine. ReviewerQueueTable.vue shows third badge.

content.subject: P9-8: listing UI — inline ProductLinePill on filter rail + ExtHero label
content.description: New ProductLinePill.vue, FilterBar plumbing, search/query.ts predicate, ExtHero.vue renders "Wireless Official" style label.

content.subject: P9-9: tests — repository, orchestrator, component, e2e
content.description: Extend reviewers + approvals integration tests; new orchestrator tests; new ReviewerMatrixProductLine unit; extend approval.spec e2e (round-trip + delegation + negative).

content.subject: P9-10: docs — ADR addendum, CLAUDE.md #12, docs/plan.md, features.md
content.description: ADR-0001 2026-06-08 addendum; CLAUDE.md decision #12 rewrite; docs/plan.md §3 + §13; docs/features.md one-liner.

import { sql } from "drizzle-orm"
import { drizzle } from "drizzle-orm/postgres-js"
import postgres from "postgres"

import { APPROVAL_REQUESTS } from "../shared/data/approval-requests"
import {
  APPROVAL_REVIEWERS,
  DEFAULT_SUPER_ADMIN_EMAIL,
} from "../shared/data/approval-reviewers"
import { COLLECTIONS } from "../shared/data/collections"
import { DEPARTMENTS } from "../shared/data/departments"
import { EXTENSIONS } from "../shared/data/extensions"
import * as schema from "../shared/db/schema"
import {
  approvalRequests,
  approvalReviewers,
  collectionItems,
  collections,
  departments,
  extensions,
  extensionTags,
  memberships,
  organizations,
  tags,
  users,
} from "../shared/db/schema"
import { TAG_LABELS } from "../shared/tags"
import type { Department } from "../shared/types"
import { seedMcpLandscape } from "./seed-mcp-landscape"

const ORG_ID = "default"

const CREATORS = [
  { id: "user-amy", email: "amy@agentcenter.dev", name: "Amy Chen" },
  { id: "user-ben", email: "ben@agentcenter.dev", name: "Ben Park" },
  { id: "user-cory", email: "cory@agentcenter.dev", name: "Cory Liu" },
  { id: "user-dao", email: "dao@agentcenter.dev", name: "Dao Tran" },
  { id: "user-eli", email: "eli@agentcenter.dev", name: "Eli Smith" },
  { id: "user-fei", email: "fei@agentcenter.dev", name: "Fei Wang" },
]

interface FlatDept {
  id: string
  orgId: string
  parentId: string | null
  name: string
  nameZh: string
  pathDepth: number
}

126	  console.log("seed: starting")
127	  console.log("seed: truncating catalog tables")
128	  // Orgs + tags CASCADE through extensions and their dependents (versions,
129	  // installs, ratings, extension_tags, approval_requests). approval_reviewers
130	  // only cascades from `users` (which we don't truncate to preserve dev
131	  // identities), so include it explicitly — otherwise a re-run hits the
132	  // appr-rev-0 PK collision below.

134	    sql`TRUNCATE TABLE ${organizations}, ${tags}, ${approvalReviewers} RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE`,
135	  )
136	
137	  const authorOrgIdMap = new Map<string, string>()
138	  const usedSlugs = new Set<string>([ORG_ID])
139	  for (const e of EXTENSIONS) {
140	    if (authorOrgIdMap.has(e.author)) continue
141	    const base = slugify(e.author) || `author-${authorOrgIdMap.size}`
142	    let slug = base
143	    for (let n = 1; usedSlugs.has(slug); n++) slug = `${base}-${n}`
144	    usedSlugs.add(slug)
145	    authorOrgIdMap.set(e.author, slug)
146	  }
147	  const orgRows = [
148	    {
149	      id: ORG_ID,
150	      slug: "default",
151	      name: "Default Organization",
152	      nameZh: "默认组织",
153	    },
154	    ...Array.from(authorOrgIdMap.entries()).map(([author, id]) => ({
155	      id,
156	      slug: id,
157	      name: author,
158	      nameZh: null,
159	    })),
160	  ]
161	  console.log(`seed: inserting ${orgRows.length} organizations`)
162	  await db.insert(organizations).values(orgRows)
163	
164	  console.log(`seed: upserting ${CREATORS.length} creator users`)
165	  await db.insert(users).values(CREATORS).onConflictDoNothing()
166	
167	  const flatDepts = flattenDepts(DEPARTMENTS)
168	  console.log(`seed: inserting ${flatDepts.length} departments`)
169	  await db.insert(departments).values(flatDepts)
170	
171	  const tagRows = Object.entries(TAG_LABELS).map(([id, label]) => ({
172	    id,
173	    labelEn: label.en,
174	    labelZh: label.zh,
175	  }))
176	  console.log(`seed: inserting ${tagRows.length} tags`)
177	  await db.insert(tags).values(tagRows)
178	
179	  const extRows = EXTENSIONS.map((e, i) => {
180	    const creator = CREATORS[i % CREATORS.length]!
181	    const ownerOrgId = authorOrgIdMap.get(e.author)
182	    if (!ownerOrgId) throw new Error(`no org for author ${e.author}`)
183	    return {
184	      id: `ext-${e.id}`,
185	      slug: slugify(e.name),
186	      category: e.category,
187	      badge: e.badge ?? null,
188	      // Demo seed only. Production extensions earn officialTier through
189	      // the approval workflow; the seed pre-stamps a handful so the new
190	      // badge styling and filter pill have visible coverage on a fresh DB.
191	      officialTier: e.officialTier ?? null,
192	      scope: e.scope,
193	      funcCat: e.funcCat,
194	      subCat: e.subCat,
195	      publisherUserId: creator.id,
196	      ownerOrgId,
197	      deptId: e.dept,
198	      iconEmoji: e.icon,
199	      iconColor: e.color,

198	      iconEmoji: e.icon,
199	      iconColor: e.color,
200	      visibility: "published" as const,
201	      name: e.name,
202	      nameZh: e.nameZh,
203	      description: e.desc,
204	      descriptionZh: e.descZh,
205	      readmeMd: generateReadme(e),
206	      downloadsCount: e.downloads,
207	      starsAvg: String(e.stars),
208	      ratingsCount: 0,
209	      publishedAt: new Date(),
210	    }
211	  })
212	  console.log(`seed: inserting ${extRows.length} extensions`)
213	  await db.insert(extensions).values(extRows)
214	
215	  const seen = new Set<string>()
216	  const membershipRows: {
217	    id: string
218	    userId: string
219	    orgId: string
220	    role: "publisher" | "superAdmin"
221	  }[] = []
222	  for (const row of extRows) {
223	    const key = `${row.publisherUserId}|${row.ownerOrgId}`
224	    if (seen.has(key)) continue
225	    seen.add(key)
226	    membershipRows.push({
227	      id: `mem-${row.publisherUserId}-${row.ownerOrgId}`,
228	      userId: row.publisherUserId,
229	      orgId: row.ownerOrgId,
230	      role: "publisher",
231	    })
232	  }
233	
234	  // Promote one user to super-admin so the /admin/reviewers matrix is
235	  // reachable in dev. SEED_SUPER_ADMIN_EMAIL overrides the default.
236	  const superAdminEmail =
237	    process.env.SEED_SUPER_ADMIN_EMAIL ?? DEFAULT_SUPER_ADMIN_EMAIL
238	  const superAdmin = CREATORS.find((u) => u.email === superAdminEmail)
239	  if (superAdmin) {
240	    membershipRows.push({
241	      id: `mem-superadmin-${superAdmin.id}`,
242	      userId: superAdmin.id,
243	      orgId: ORG_ID,
244	      role: "superAdmin",
245	    })
246	    console.log(`seed: promoting ${superAdmin.email} to superAdmin`)
247	  } else {
248	    console.warn(
249	      `seed: SEED_SUPER_ADMIN_EMAIL=${superAdminEmail} did not match any creator — no super-admin seeded`,
250	    )
251	  }
252	  console.log(`seed: inserting ${membershipRows.length} memberships`)
253	  await db.insert(memberships).values(membershipRows)
254	
255	  // Reviewer matrix: every (tier, subCat) cell gets at least one
256	  // assignment from APPROVAL_REVIEWERS. Emails that don't resolve to a
257	  // creator are skipped with a warning so a typo doesn't break the seed.
258	  const reviewerRows = APPROVAL_REVIEWERS.flatMap((r, i) => {
259	    const reviewer = CREATORS.find((u) => u.email === r.reviewerEmail)
260	    if (!reviewer) {
261	      console.warn(
262	        `seed: approval reviewer email ${r.reviewerEmail} did not match any creator — skipping`,
263	      )
264	      return []
265	    }
266	    return [
267	      {
268	        id: `appr-rev-${i}`,
269	        tier: r.tier,
270	        subCat: r.subCat,
271	        userId: reviewer.id,
272	      },
273	    ]
274	  })
275	  console.log(`seed: inserting ${reviewerRows.length} approval reviewers`)
276	  await db.insert(approvalReviewers).values(reviewerRows)
277	
278	  const extTagRows = EXTENSIONS.flatMap((e) =>
279	    e.tags.map((tagKey) => ({
280	      extensionId: `ext-${e.id}`,
281	      tagId: tagKey,
282	    })),
283	  )
284	  console.log(`seed: inserting ${extTagRows.length} extension-tag links`)
285	  await db.insert(extensionTags).values(extTagRows)
286	
287	  // Email and slug → id maps shared by the approval-request block below
288	  // and the editorial-collections block further down. Hoisted here so
289	  // both sections look up against the same table.
290	  const userIdByEmail = new Map(CREATORS.map((u) => [u.email, u.id]))
291	  const extIdBySlug = new Map(extRows.map((r) => [r.slug, r.id]))
292

export type Locale = "en" | "zh"

export type Theme = "ivory" | "dark" | "mono"

export type ExtensionCategory = "skills" | "mcp" | "slash" | "plugins"
export type ExtensionScope = "personal" | "org" | "enterprise"
export type ExtensionBadge = "official" | "popular" | "new"
export type FuncCatKey = "workTask" | "business" | "tools"

export interface FuncTaxonomyL1 {
  key: string
  l2: string[]
}

export interface FuncTaxonomyNode {
  key: FuncCatKey
  l1: FuncTaxonomyL1[]
}

export interface TagLabel {
  en: string
  zh: string
}

export interface Department {
  id: string
  name: string
  nameZh: string
  children?: Department[]
}

export interface Extension {
  id: number
  dept: string
  tags: string[]
  name: string
  nameZh: string
  author: string
  category: ExtensionCategory
  badge: ExtensionBadge | null
  // Optional seed-side stamp. Lets the dev seed pre-approve a handful of
  // extensions so the new tier badge / filter rail has visible data on a
  // fresh DB. Production rows pick this up from the approval workflow,
  // never from this field.
  officialTier?: "productLine" | "company" | null
  downloads: number
  stars: number
  color: string
  icon: string
  funcCat: FuncCatKey
  subCat: string
  scope: ExtensionScope
  desc: string
  descZh: string
}

export interface CreatorFacet {
  id: string
  name: string | null
  email: string
  count: number
}

export interface PublisherFacet {
  id: string
  name: string
  nameZh: string | null
  slug: string
  count: number
}

export interface TagFacet {
  id: string
  labelEn: string
  labelZh: string
  count: number
}

export interface ProfileStats {
  installedCount: number
  publishedCount: number
  totalInstallsOfMine: number
  avgRatingOfMine: number | null
}

export interface ProfileInstalledRow {
  extensionId: string
  slug: string
  name: string
  category: string
  iconColor: string | null
  installedVersion: string
  installedAt: string
}

export interface ProfileSavedRow {
  extensionId: string
  slug: string
  name: string
  category: string
  iconColor: string | null
  savedAt: string
}

export interface ProfileDraftRow {
  extensionId: string
  slug: string
  name: string
  category: string
  iconColor: string | null
  updatedAt: string
  latestStatus: string | null
}

export interface ProfilePublishedRow {
  extensionId: string
  slug: string
  name: string
  category: string
  iconColor: string | null
  latestVersion: string | null
  downloadsCount: number
  starsAvg: string
  ratingsCount: number
}

export type ProfileActivityEvent =
  | { kind: "installed"; at: string; extensionId: string; slug: string; name: string; version: string }
  | { kind: "published"; at: string; extensionId: string; slug: string; name: string; version: string }
  | { kind: "rated"; at: string; extensionId: string; slug: string; name: string; stars: number }

export interface ProfileRequestRow {
  requestId: string
  extensionId: string
  slug: string
  name: string
  category: string
  iconColor: string | null
  requestedTier: "productLine" | "company"
  subCat: string
  status: "pending" | "approved" | "rejected" | "withdrawn"
  reason: string | null
  reviewerNote: string | null
  createdAt: string
  decidedAt: string | null
}

export interface ProfileCollectionRow {
  id: string
  slug: string
  name: string
  nameZh: string | null
  description: string | null
  descriptionZh: string | null
  systemKind: "installed" | "saved" | null
  visibility: "private" | "public"
  itemCount: number
  updatedAt: string
}

lines: -  // Reviewer matrix: every (tier, subCat) cell gets at least one

-  // creator are skipped with a warning so a typo doesn't break the seed.
+  // Reviewer matrix: every (tier, subCat[, productLineId]) cell gets at least
+  // one assignment from APPROVAL_REVIEWERS. Emails that don't resolve to a
+  // creator are skipped with a warning so a typo doesn't break the seed. The
+  // DB CHECK enforces productLineId presence per tier; we mirror it here so a
+  // bad shape in the seed file fails loudly instead of at INSERT time.
+        `seed: approval reviewer #${i} (${r.tier}/${r.subCat}) carries productLineId on a company-tier row — skipping`,

    const now = new Date()
    const extRows = CATALOG.map((entry) => ({

      slug: entry.slug,
      category: entry.category,
      scope: entry.scope,
      funcCat: entry.funcCat,

      downloadsCount: entry.downloadsCount,

    await tx

content.subject: P9-1: schema + migration + seed for product_lines and product_line_id
content.description: Drizzle schema additions (product_lines table; product_line_id on approval_reviewers, approval_requests, extensions; CHECK constraints; partial unique indexes). Generate migration 0009; add inline seed for the 4 product lines. Update db seed script if needed.

content.subject: P9-2: repo layer — thread productLineId through reviewers + approvals + new productLines repo
content.description: server/repositories/reviewers.ts (listMatrix, listCellsForUser, findReviewersFor, insertReviewer, isCompanyAdminForSubCat). server/repositories/approvals.ts (ApprovalRequestRow, listPendingForCells, isReviewerForCell, setExtensionOfficialTier). New server/repositories/productLines.ts.

content.subject: P9-3: orchestrator — productLine-aware submit/decide + new error codes
content.description: server/utils/approvals.ts SubmitParams gains productLineId; iff-rule; decideRequest passes productLineId; new error codes missing_product_line and not_company_admin.

content.subject: P9-4: validators — require productLineId iff tier=productLine
content.description: SubmitApprovalSchema, AssignReviewerSchema, filtersSchema (shared/validators/).

content.subject: P9-5: API — requireCellAdmin + /api/internal/product-lines + relaxed reviewers GET
content.description: New requireCellAdmin helper. assign/unassign use it. reviewers/index.get returns canEdit, viewer, productLines. submit honours productLineId. /api/v1 contract: confirm unchanged, add explanatory comment.

content.subject: P9-6: admin UI — split ReviewerMatrix into Company + ProductLine subviews
content.description: ReviewerMatrixCompany.vue, ReviewerMatrixProductLine.vue, tabbed shell, canEdit greying, URL hash persistence.

content.subject: P9-7: publish UI — productLine picker in RequestOfficialDialog + queue badge
content.description: RequestOfficialDialog.vue gains productLine select when tier=productLine. ReviewerQueueTable.vue shows third badge.

content.subject: P9-8: listing UI — inline ProductLinePill on filter rail + ExtHero label
content.description: New ProductLinePill.vue, FilterBar plumbing, search/query.ts predicate, ExtHero.vue renders "Wireless Official" style label.

content.subject: P9-9: tests — repository, orchestrator, component, e2e
content.description: Extend reviewers + approvals integration tests; new orchestrator tests; new ReviewerMatrixProductLine unit; extend approval.spec e2e (round-trip + delegation + negative).

content.subject: P9-10: docs — ADR addendum, CLAUDE.md #12, docs/plan.md, features.md
content.description: ADR-0001 2026-06-08 addendum; CLAUDE.md decision #12 rewrite; docs/plan.md §3 + §13; docs/features.md one-liner.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(p9-db): add product_lines table and product_line_id columns

Introduces the productLine dimension for the reviewer matrix. The seeded
product_lines table (wireless, datacom, terminals, cloud) feeds three new
nullable product_line_id columns on approval_reviewers, approval_requests,
and extensions. A CHECK constraint on each table enforces the shape rule:
productLineId is present iff the corresponding tier is 'productLine'.

The approval_reviewers uniqueness rule splits into two partial unique
indexes — productLine cells include productLineId, company cells do not —
which is clearer than relying on NULLS NOT DISTINCT and keeps each tier's
arity self-documenting.

Seed data (APPROVAL_REVIEWERS, APPROVAL_REQUESTS, catalog catalog rows)
threads the new column through. Pre-existing productLine-tier reviewer
rows fan out to one row per product line so coverage is preserved without
picking favourites; legacy productLine extensions/requests default to
'wireless'.
EOF
)"

1	import { and, asc, eq } from "drizzle-orm"
2	
3	import type { OfficialTier } from "~~/shared/approvals/state"
4	import {
5	  approvalReviewers,
6	  memberships,
7	  users,
8	} from "~~/shared/db/schema"
9	
10	import type { Transactable } from "./types"
11	
12	// `approval_reviewers` table accessor. Backs the reviewer matrix:
13	// (tier × subCat) → list of users assigned to decide on that cell.
14	// Also exposes the super-admin lookup used by the admin guards.
15	
16	export type { OfficialTier }
17	
18	export interface ReviewerRow {
19	  id: string
20	  tier: OfficialTier
21	  subCat: string
22	  userId: string
23	  userEmail: string
24	  userName: string | null
25	  createdAt: Date
26	}
27	
28	export interface InsertReviewerRow {
29	  id: string
30	  tier: OfficialTier
31	  subCat: string
32	  userId: string
33	}
34	
35	// Full matrix view, joined with `users` so the matrix UI can render
36	// reviewer chips with a recognisable label without an extra round-trip.
37	export async function listMatrix(db: Transactable): Promise<ReviewerRow[]> {
38	  return db
39	    .select({
40	      id: approvalReviewers.id,
41	      tier: approvalReviewers.tier,
42	      subCat: approvalReviewers.subCat,
43	      userId: approvalReviewers.userId,
44	      userEmail: users.email,
45	      userName: users.name,
46	      createdAt: approvalReviewers.createdAt,
47	    })
48	    .from(approvalReviewers)
49	    .innerJoin(users, eq(users.id, approvalReviewers.userId))
50	    .orderBy(
51	      asc(approvalReviewers.tier),
52	      asc(approvalReviewers.subCat),
53	      asc(users.email),
54	    )
55	}
56	
57	// Cells (tier × subCat) where the given user is assigned. Used by the
58	// orchestrator to drive `listPendingForCells` from `approvals.ts`.
59	export async function listCellsForUser(
60	  db: Transactable,
61	  userId: string,
62	): Promise<Array<{ tier: OfficialTier; subCat: string }>> {
63	  return db
64	    .select({
65	      tier: approvalReviewers.tier,
66	      subCat: approvalReviewers.subCat,
67	    })
68	    .from(approvalReviewers)
69	    .where(eq(approvalReviewers.userId, userId))
70	}
71	
72	// Per-cell reviewer list — useful for the orchestrator's notification fan-out.
73	export async function findReviewersFor(
74	  db: Transactable,
75	  tier: OfficialTier,
76	  subCat: string,
77	): Promise<string[]> {
78	  const rows = await db
79	    .select({ userId: approvalReviewers.userId })
80	    .from(approvalReviewers)
81	    .where(
82	      and(
83	        eq(approvalReviewers.tier, tier),
84	        eq(approvalReviewers.subCat, subCat),
85	      ),
86	    )
87	  return rows.map((r) => r.userId)
88	}
89	
90	export async function insertReviewer(
91	  db: Transactable,
92	  row: InsertReviewerRow,
93	): Promise<void> {
94	  await db.insert(approvalReviewers).values(row).onConflictDoNothing({
95	    target: [
96	      approvalReviewers.tier,
97	      approvalReviewers.subCat,
98	      approvalReviewers.userId,
99	    ],
100	  })
101	}
102	
103	export async function deleteReviewer(
104	  db: Transactable,
105	  id: string,
106	): Promise<void> {
107	  await db.delete(approvalReviewers).where(eq(approvalReviewers.id, id))
108	}
109	
110	// Super-admin check. A user is a super-admin if any of their memberships
111	// carries the `superAdmin` role. Decoupled from a specific org so seeded
112	// super-admins keep their authority even when the default org is renamed.
113	export async function isSuperAdmin(
114	  db: Transactable,
115	  userId: string,
116	): Promise<boolean> {
117	  const [row] = await db
118	    .select({ id: memberships.id })
119	    .from(memberships)
120	    .where(
121	      and(
122	        eq(memberships.userId, userId),
123	        eq(memberships.role, "superAdmin"),
124	      ),
125	    )
126	    .limit(1)
127	  return !!row
128	}

1	import { and, desc, eq, inArray, sql } from "drizzle-orm"
2	
3	import type {
4	  ApprovalStatus,
5	  OfficialTier,
6	} from "~~/shared/approvals/state"
7	import {
8	  approvalRequests,
9	  approvalReviewers,
10	  extensions,
11	} from "~~/shared/db/schema"
12	
13	import type { Transactable } from "./types"
14	
15	// `approval_requests` table accessor. Used by the approval orchestrator to
16	// drive the publisher submit / reviewer decide / publisher withdraw flow.
17	// `ApprovalStatus` and `OfficialTier` are re-exported so callers that
18	// already depend on the repository module don't need a second import.
19	
20	export type { ApprovalStatus, OfficialTier }
21	
22	export interface ApprovalRequestRow {
23	  id: string
24	  extensionId: string
25	  requestedTier: OfficialTier
26	  subCat: string
27	  requestedByUserId: string
28	  reason: string | null
29	  status: ApprovalStatus
30	  decidedByUserId: string | null
31	  decidedAt: Date | null
32	  reviewerNote: string | null
33	  createdAt: Date
34	  updatedAt: Date
35	}
36	
37	const fullSelect = {
38	  id: approvalRequests.id,
39	  extensionId: approvalRequests.extensionId,
40	  requestedTier: approvalRequests.requestedTier,
41	  subCat: approvalRequests.subCat,
42	  requestedByUserId: approvalRequests.requestedByUserId,
43	  reason: approvalRequests.reason,
44	  status: approvalRequests.status,
45	  decidedByUserId: approvalRequests.decidedByUserId,
46	  decidedAt: approvalRequests.decidedAt,
47	  reviewerNote: approvalRequests.reviewerNote,
48	  createdAt: approvalRequests.createdAt,
49	  updatedAt: approvalRequests.updatedAt,
50	}
51	
52	export interface InsertApprovalRequest {
53	  id: string
54	  extensionId: string
55	  requestedTier: OfficialTier
56	  subCat: string
57	  requestedByUserId: string
58	  reason: string | null
59	}
60	
61	export async function insertRequest(
62	  db: Transactable,
63	  row: InsertApprovalRequest,
64	): Promise<ApprovalRequestRow> {
65	  const [created] = await db
66	    .insert(approvalRequests)
67	    .values(row)
68	    .returning(fullSelect)
69	  if (!created) throw new Error("approval request insert vanished")
70	  return created
71	}
72	
73	export async function findById(
74	  db: Transactable,
75	  id: string,
76	): Promise<ApprovalRequestRow | null> {
77	  const [row] = await db
78	    .select(fullSelect)
79	    .from(approvalRequests)
80	    .where(eq(approvalRequests.id, id))
81	    .limit(1)
82	  return row ?? null
83	}
84	
85	// Bulk variant of `findPendingByExtension` for the publisher dashboard —
86	// the listing already loads many extensions, so we fan in a single query
87	// rather than N round-trips and return a Map<extId, pendingRequest>.
88	export async function findPendingForExtensions(
89	  db: Transactable,
90	  extensionIds: string[],
91	): Promise<Map<string, ApprovalRequestRow>> {
92	  if (extensionIds.length === 0) return new Map()
93	  const rows = await db
94	    .select(fullSelect)
95	    .from(approvalRequests)
96	    .where(
97	      and(
98	        inArray(approvalRequests.extensionId, extensionIds),
99	        eq(approvalRequests.status, "pending"),
100	      ),
101	    )
102	  return new Map(rows.map((r) => [r.extensionId, r]))
103	}
104	
105	// Used by the at-most-one-pending guard in the submit orchestrator.
106	export async function findPendingByExtension(
107	  db: Transactable,
108	  extensionId: string,
109	): Promise<ApprovalRequestRow | null> {
110	  const [row] = await db
111	    .select(fullSelect)
112	    .from(approvalRequests)
113	    .where(
114	      and(
115	        eq(approvalRequests.extensionId, extensionId),
116	        eq(approvalRequests.status, "pending"),
117	      ),
118	    )
119	    .limit(1)
120	  return row ?? null
121	}
122	
123	// Publisher's "my requests" view.
124	export async function listForPublisher(
125	  db: Transactable,
126	  userId: string,
127	): Promise<ApprovalRequestRow[]> {
128	  return db
129	    .select(fullSelect)
130	    .from(approvalRequests)
131	    .where(eq(approvalRequests.requestedByUserId, userId))
132	    .orderBy(desc(approvalRequests.createdAt))
133	}
134	
135	// Reviewer's queue — pending requests in any (tier, subCat) cell that the
136	// reviewer is assigned to. Implemented as a single query against the cells
137	// the reviewer covers; the orchestrator pre-computes the cell list from
138	// `approval_reviewers` to keep this method driver-portable (no LATERAL).
139	export async function listPendingForCells(
140	  db: Transactable,
141	  cells: Array<{ tier: OfficialTier; subCat: string }>,
142	): Promise<ApprovalRequestRow[]> {
143	  if (cells.length === 0) return []
144	  // Build an OR over (tier=, subCat=) pairs. Postgres collapses these into
145	  // a single index scan against idx_approval_status_cell.
146	  const cellMatches = cells.map((c) =>
147	    and(
148	      eq(approvalRequests.requestedTier, c.tier),
149	      eq(approvalRequests.subCat, c.subCat),
150	    ),
151	  )
152	  return db
153	    .select(fullSelect)
154	    .from(approvalRequests)
155	    .where(
156	      and(
157	        eq(approvalRequests.status, "pending"),
158	        sql`(${sql.join(cellMatches, sql` OR `)})`,
159	      ),
160	    )
161	    .orderBy(desc(approvalRequests.createdAt))
162	}
163	
164	export interface DecidePatch {
165	  status: "approved" | "rejected"
166	  decidedByUserId: string
167	  decidedAt: Date
168	  reviewerNote: string | null
169	}
170	
171	// Optimistic-locking variant: only updates a request that's still pending.
172	// Returns the number of affected rows so the orchestrator can detect a
173	// race (a second reviewer that committed between the orchestrator's
174	// pre-check and this UPDATE) and surface it as `request_not_pending`.
175	export async function applyDecision(
176	  db: Transactable,
177	  id: string,
178	  patch: DecidePatch,
179	): Promise<number> {
180	  const updated = await db
181	    .update(approvalRequests)
182	    .set({ ...patch, updatedAt: new Date() })
183	    .where(
184	      and(
185	        eq(approvalRequests.id, id),
186	        eq(approvalRequests.status, "pending"),
187	      ),
188	    )
189	    .returning({ id: approvalRequests.id })
190	  return updated.length
191	}
192	
193	// Same optimistic-locking shape as `applyDecision` — only a pending
194	// request can be withdrawn, and the affected row count tells the caller
195	// whether the transition actually fired.
196	export async function applyWithdraw(
197	  db: Transactable,
198	  id: string,
199	  decidedAt: Date,
200	): Promise<number> {
201	  const updated = await db
202	    .update(approvalRequests)
203	    .set({ status: "withdrawn", decidedAt, updatedAt: new Date() })
204	    .where(
205	      and(
206	        eq(approvalRequests.id, id),
207	        eq(approvalRequests.status, "pending"),
208	      ),
209	    )
210	    .returning({ id: approvalRequests.id })
211	  return updated.length
212	}
213	
214	// Companion mutator for the approve path — stamps `officialTier` on the
215	// parent extension. Lives here (rather than in `extensions.ts`) because
216	// the approval orchestrator is the only legitimate writer; keeping the
217	// call adjacent to the request mutation makes that intent obvious.
218	export async function setExtensionOfficialTier(
219	  db: Transactable,
220	  extensionId: string,
221	  tier: OfficialTier,
222	): Promise<void> {
223	  await db
224	    .update(extensions)
225	    .set({ officialTier: tier, updatedAt: new Date() })
226	    .where(eq(extensions.id, extensionId))
227	}
228	
229	// Bulk lookup of `officialTier` for a set of extensions — used by the
230	// publisher dashboard to render the per-row badge alongside any pending
231	// requests in one round-trip.
232	export async function findTiersForExtensions(
233	  db: Transactable,
234	  extensionIds: string[],
235	): Promise<Map<string, OfficialTier | null>> {
236	  if (extensionIds.length === 0) return new Map()
237	  const rows = await db
238	    .select({ id: extensions.id, officialTier: extensions.officialTier })
239	    .from(extensions)
240	    .where(inArray(extensions.id, extensionIds))
241	  return new Map(rows.map((r) => [r.id, r.officialTier ?? null]))
242	}
243	
244	// Used by guards in `decide.post.ts` — confirms a user is assigned to the
245	// cell that the request originated from. Super-admins are handled in the
246	// orchestrator (a separate join) so this stays a pure reviewer check.
247	export async function isReviewerForCell(
248	  db: Transactable,
249	  userId: string,
250	  tier: OfficialTier,
251	  subCat: string,
252	): Promise<boolean> {
253	  const [row] = await db
254	    .select({ id: approvalReviewers.id })
255	    .from(approvalReviewers)
256	    .where(
257	      and(
258	        eq(approvalReviewers.userId, userId),
259	        eq(approvalReviewers.tier, tier),
260	        eq(approvalReviewers.subCat, subCat),
261	      ),
262	    )
263	    .limit(1)
264	  return !!row
265	}
266

+// SubCats this user is a company-tier reviewer for. Drives the delegated
+// matrix-edit gate: a company admin of subCat X can manage productLine
+// reviewers for subCat X (and only X).
+// Cheap per-cell version of the above for endpoint authorisation paths.
// Per-cell reviewer list — useful for the orchestrator's notification fan-out.
+// For company tier the productLineId argument is ignored (and asserted null
+// in callers); for productLine tier it must be supplied or the cell match is
+  // No `target` on the conflict clause — the table now carries two partial
+  // unique indexes (one per tier) and Drizzle can't synthesise a typed
+  // target across both. The CHECK + partial uniques still de-dupe at the
+  // DB layer; this just makes a duplicate insert a no-op instead of an error.
+// Single-row fetch used by the unassign endpoint so it can authorise
+// against the row's tier/subCat (delegation rule) before deleting.
// Super-admin check. A user is a super-admin if any of their memberships
// carries the `superAdmin` role. Decoupled from a specific org so seeded
// super-admins keep their authority even when the default org is renamed.

1	import * as approvalsRepo from "~~/server/repositories/approvals"
2	import * as extensionsRepo from "~~/server/repositories/extensions"
3	import * as reviewersRepo from "~~/server/repositories/reviewers"
4	import {
5	  decideApprovalOutcome,
6	  decideWithdrawOutcome,
7	  type ApprovalAction,
8	  type OfficialTier,
9	} from "~~/shared/approvals/state"
10	
11	import { useDb } from "./db"
12	import { inngest } from "./inngest"
13	
14	// Orchestrators over the approvals/reviewers/extensions repositories. Pure
15	// transitions live in `shared/approvals/state.ts`; this file loads rows,
16	// opens transactions, fires Inngest events, and maps domain errors to
17	// HTTP-shaped codes the endpoints translate.
18	
19	export type ApprovalErrorCode =
20	  | "extension_not_found"
21	  | "not_publisher_owner"
22	  | "extension_not_published"
23	  | "request_not_found"
24	  | "request_not_pending"
25	  | "duplicate_pending_request"
26	  | "not_reviewer"
27	  | "not_requester"
28	
29	export class ApprovalError extends Error {
30	  readonly code: ApprovalErrorCode
31	  constructor(code: ApprovalErrorCode) {
32	    super(code)
33	    this.code = code
34	    this.name = "ApprovalError"
35	  }
36	}
37	
38	export interface SubmitParams {
39	  extensionId: string
40	  requestedTier: OfficialTier
41	  subCat: string
42	  userId: string
43	  reason: string | undefined
44	}
45	
46	export async function submitRequest(
47	  params: SubmitParams,
48	): Promise<approvalsRepo.ApprovalRequestRow> {
49	  const db = useDb()
50	
51	  const ext = await extensionsRepo.findById(db, params.extensionId)
52	  if (!ext) throw new ApprovalError("extension_not_found")
53	  if (ext.publisherUserId !== params.userId) {
54	    throw new ApprovalError("not_publisher_owner")
55	  }
56	  if (ext.visibility !== "published") {
57	    throw new ApprovalError("extension_not_published")
58	  }
59	
60	  const row = await db.transaction(async (tx) => {
61	    // Re-check inside the transaction — race window between the visibility
62	    // load above and the insert below would otherwise let a duplicate slip
63	    // through. PG isolation is `read committed` so the second check still
64	    // sees a concurrent insert that already committed.
65	    const existing = await approvalsRepo.findPendingByExtension(
66	      tx,
67	      params.extensionId,
68	    )
69	    if (existing) throw new ApprovalError("duplicate_pending_request")
70	
71	    return approvalsRepo.insertRequest(tx, {
72	      id: crypto.randomUUID(),
73	      extensionId: params.extensionId,
74	      requestedTier: params.requestedTier,
75	      subCat: params.subCat,
76	      requestedByUserId: params.userId,
77	      reason: params.reason ?? null,
78	    })
79	  })
80	
81	  await inngest.send({
82	    name: "extension/approval.requested",
83	    data: {
84	      requestId: row.id,
85	      extensionId: row.extensionId,
86	      requestedTier: row.requestedTier,
87	      subCat: row.subCat,
88	      requesterUserId: row.requestedByUserId,
89	    },
90	  })
91	
92	  return row
93	}
94	
95	export interface DecideParams {
96	  requestId: string
97	  action: ApprovalAction
98	  reviewerUserId: string
99	}
100	
101	export async function decideRequest(
102	  params: DecideParams,
103	): Promise<approvalsRepo.ApprovalRequestRow> {
104	  const db = useDb()
105	
106	  const current = await approvalsRepo.findById(db, params.requestId)
107	  if (!current) throw new ApprovalError("request_not_found")
108	  if (current.status !== "pending") {
109	    throw new ApprovalError("request_not_pending")
110	  }
111	
112	  // Super-admins can decide on any cell; otherwise the reviewer must be
113	  // assigned to (requestedTier, subCat) on this request.
114	  const allowed =
115	    (await reviewersRepo.isSuperAdmin(db, params.reviewerUserId)) ||
116	    (await approvalsRepo.isReviewerForCell(
117	      db,
118	      params.reviewerUserId,
119	      current.requestedTier,
120	      current.subCat,
121	    ))
122	  if (!allowed) throw new ApprovalError("not_reviewer")
123	
124	  const outcome = decideApprovalOutcome({
125	    current: {
126	      status: current.status,
127	      requestedTier: current.requestedTier,
128	    },
129	    action: params.action,
130	    reviewerUserId: params.reviewerUserId,
131	    now: new Date(),
132	  })
133	
134	  // Optimistic locking lives inside the repository's UPDATE — if a second
135	  // reviewer decided between the pre-check above and this transaction, the
136	  // affected row count comes back zero and we surface the race as
137	  // `request_not_pending`.
138	  await db.transaction(async (tx) => {
139	    const updated = await approvalsRepo.applyDecision(
140	      tx,
141	      params.requestId,
142	      outcome.request,
143	    )
144	    if (updated === 0) throw new ApprovalError("request_not_pending")
145	    if (outcome.extension) {
146	      await approvalsRepo.setExtensionOfficialTier(
147	        tx,
148	        current.extensionId,
149	        outcome.extension.officialTier,
150	      )
151	    }
152	  })
153	
154	  const updated = await approvalsRepo.findById(db, params.requestId)
155	  if (!updated) throw new Error("approval request vanished mid-decide")
156	
157	  await inngest.send({
158	    name: "extension/approval.decided",
159	    data: {
160	      requestId: updated.id,
161	      extensionId: updated.extensionId,
162	      decision: outcome.request.status,
163	      decidedByUserId: params.reviewerUserId,
164	    },
165	  })
166	
167	  return updated
168	}
169	
170	export interface WithdrawParams {
171	  requestId: string
172	  userId: string
173	}
174	
175	export async function withdrawRequest(
176	  params: WithdrawParams,
177	): Promise<approvalsRepo.ApprovalRequestRow> {
178	  const db = useDb()
179	
180	  const current = await approvalsRepo.findById(db, params.requestId)
181	  if (!current) throw new ApprovalError("request_not_found")
182	  if (current.requestedByUserId !== params.userId) {
183	    throw new ApprovalError("not_requester")
184	  }
185	  if (current.status !== "pending") {
186	    throw new ApprovalError("request_not_pending")
187	  }
188	
189	  const outcome = decideWithdrawOutcome({
190	    current: { status: current.status },
191	    now: new Date(),
192	  })
193	  const affected = await approvalsRepo.applyWithdraw(
194	    db,
195	    params.requestId,
196	    outcome.request.decidedAt,
197	  )
198	  if (affected === 0) throw new ApprovalError("request_not_pending")
199	
200	  const updated = await approvalsRepo.findById(db, params.requestId)
201	  if (!updated) throw new Error("approval request vanished mid-withdraw")
202	  return updated
203	}
204	
205	// Reviewer queue — pending requests in any cell this user covers. Returns
206	// an empty array when the user has no cell assignments (and is not a
207	// super-admin); super-admins see the whole pending queue.
208	export async function listReviewerQueue(
209	  userId: string,
210	): Promise<approvalsRepo.ApprovalRequestRow[]> {
211	  const db = useDb()
212	  if (await reviewersRepo.isSuperAdmin(db, userId)) {
213	    // Treat super-admin as "assigned to every cell". Pulled from the
214	    // matrix verbatim so an empty matrix still surfaces no rows — better
215	    // than a silent firehose.
216	    const cells = await reviewersRepo
217	      .listMatrix(db)
218	      .then((rows) =>
219	        Array.from(
220	          new Set(rows.map((r) => `${r.tier}:${r.subCat}`)),
221	        ).map((k) => {
222	          const [tier, subCat] = k.split(":") as [OfficialTier, string]
223	          return { tier, subCat }
224	        }),
225	      )
226	    return approvalsRepo.listPendingForCells(db, cells)
227	  }
228	
229	  const cells = await reviewersRepo.listCellsForUser(db, userId)
230	  return approvalsRepo.listPendingForCells(db, cells)
231	}
232	
233	export async function listPublisherRequests(
234	  userId: string,
235	): Promise<approvalsRepo.ApprovalRequestRow[]> {
236	  return approvalsRepo.listForPublisher(useDb(), userId)
237	}
238

8	  )

1	import {
2	  isSuperAdmin,
3	  listCellsForUser,
4	} from "~~/server/repositories/reviewers"
5	
6	// Lightweight "what can I do in the admin surface" probe — backs the
7	// require-reviewer / require-super-admin client middleware so the pages
8	// can fail fast with 403 → redirect rather than rendering and then
9	// hitting an empty page.
10	export default defineEventHandler(async (event) => {
11	  const user = await requireUser(event)
12	  const db = useDb()
13	  const [superAdmin, cells] = await Promise.all([
14	    isSuperAdmin(db, user.id),
15	    listCellsForUser(db, user.id),
16	  ])
17	  return {
18	    isSuperAdmin: superAdmin,
19	    isReviewer: superAdmin || cells.length > 0,
20	  }
21	})

content.content:
1	import { z } from "zod"
2	
3	import { FUNC_TAXONOMY } from "~~/shared/taxonomy"
4	
5	// SubCat = the l1 leaf inside FUNC_TAXONOMY (e.g. "systemDesign",
6	// "softDev", "network", "embedded"). Flatten at module load so the
7	// validator runs O(1) without re-traversing the constant on each call.
8	// `SUB_CAT_KEY_LIST` is derived from the Set so the two stay in sync.
9	const SUB_CAT_KEYS = new Set<string>(
10	  FUNC_TAXONOMY.flatMap((c) => c.l1.map((s) => s.key)),
11	)
12	
13	export const SUB_CAT_KEY_LIST: readonly string[] = [...SUB_CAT_KEYS]
14	
15	export const OfficialTier = z.enum(["productLine", "company"])
16	export type OfficialTier = z.infer<typeof OfficialTier>
17	
18	const SubCatKey = z.string().refine((v) => SUB_CAT_KEYS.has(v), {
19	  message: "must be a key from FUNC_TAXONOMY",
20	})
21	
22	export const APPROVAL_REASON_MAX = 500
23	export const APPROVAL_NOTE_MAX = 500
24	
25	const optionalText = (max: number) =>
26	  z
27	    .string()
28	    .trim()
29	    .max(max)
30	    .optional()
31	    .transform((v) => (v && v.length > 0 ? v : undefined))
32	
33	export const SubmitApprovalSchema = z.object({
34	  extensionId: z.string().trim().min(1),
35	  requestedTier: OfficialTier,
36	  subCat: SubCatKey,
37	  reason: optionalText(APPROVAL_REASON_MAX),
38	})
39	export type SubmitApprovalInput = z.infer<typeof SubmitApprovalSchema>
40	
41	// Discriminated union: a reviewer note only makes sense on a rejection.
42	// The orchestrator drops any note sent with an approve action anyway —
43	// making that explicit at the API boundary catches the mistake before
44	// it crosses the wire instead of silently after.
45	export const DecideApprovalSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("decision", [
46	  z.object({
47	    requestId: z.string().trim().min(1),
48	    decision: z.literal("approve"),
49	  }),
50	  z.object({
51	    requestId: z.string().trim().min(1),
52	    decision: z.literal("reject"),
53	    note: optionalText(APPROVAL_NOTE_MAX),
54	  }),
55	])
56	export type DecideApprovalInput = z.infer<typeof DecideApprovalSchema>
57	
58	export const WithdrawApprovalSchema = z.object({
59	  requestId: z.string().trim().min(1),
60	})
61	export type WithdrawApprovalInput = z.infer<typeof WithdrawApprovalSchema>
62	
63	export const AssignReviewerSchema = z.object({
64	  tier: OfficialTier,
65	  subCat: SubCatKey,
66	  userId: z.string().trim().min(1),
67	})
68	export type AssignReviewerInput = z.infer<typeof AssignReviewerSchema>
69	
70	export const UnassignReviewerSchema = z.object({
71	  id: z.string().trim().min(1),
72	})
73	export type UnassignReviewerInput = z.infer<typeof UnassignReviewerSchema>
74	
75	export const ListApprovalsQuerySchema = z.object({
76	  // "mine"  → requests this user submitted as a publisher
77	  // "queue" → pending requests routed to this user as a reviewer
78	  view: z.enum(["mine", "queue"]).default("mine"),
79	})
80	export type ListApprovalsQuery = z.infer<typeof ListApprovalsQuerySchema>
81

lines: message: "must be a key from FUNC_TAXONOMY",
+// Product-line ids are seeded text PKs in kebab-case (wireless, datacom,
+// terminals, cloud as of 0009). Validate the shape here; the DB FK + CHECK
+// constraint enforces membership and the iff-rule against the actual tier.
+    message: "must be a kebab-case product-line id",
+// iff-rule helpers — the DB CHECK is the source of truth, but surfacing
+// the rule at the validator layer turns a bad request into a typed Zod
+// error instead of a generic 23514 from Postgres.

1	import {
2	  ApprovalError,
3	  submitRequest,
4	} from "~~/server/utils/approvals"
5	import { SubmitApprovalSchema } from "~~/shared/validators/approvals"
6	
7	const ERROR_STATUS: Record<string, number> = {
8	  extension_not_found: 404,
9	  not_publisher_owner: 403,
10	  extension_not_published: 409,
11	  duplicate_pending_request: 409,
12	}
13	
14	export default defineEventHandler(async (event) => {
15	  const user = await requireUser(event)
16	  const body = await readValidatedBody(event, (raw) =>
17	    SubmitApprovalSchema.parse(raw),
18	  )
19	
20	  try {
21	    const row = await submitRequest({
22	      extensionId: body.extensionId,
23	      requestedTier: body.requestedTier,
24	      subCat: body.subCat,
25	      userId: user.id,
26	      reason: body.reason,
27	    })
28	    return { ok: true, request: row }
29	  } catch (err) {
30	    if (err instanceof ApprovalError) {
31	      const status = ERROR_STATUS[err.code] ?? 400
32	      throw createError({ statusCode: status, statusMessage: err.code })
33	    }
34	    console.error("internal submitRequest failed", err)
35	    throw createError({
36	      statusCode: 500,
37	      statusMessage: "approval_submit_failed",
38	    })
39	  }
40	})
41

content.subject: P9-1: schema + migration + seed for product_lines and product_line_id
content.description: Drizzle schema additions (product_lines table; product_line_id on approval_reviewers, approval_requests, extensions; CHECK constraints; partial unique indexes). Generate migration 0009; add inline seed for the 4 product lines. Update db seed script if needed.

content.subject: P9-2: repo layer — thread productLineId through reviewers + approvals + new productLines repo
content.description: server/repositories/reviewers.ts (listMatrix, listCellsForUser, findReviewersFor, insertReviewer, isCompanyAdminForSubCat). server/repositories/approvals.ts (ApprovalRequestRow, listPendingForCells, isReviewerForCell, setExtensionOfficialTier). New server/repositories/productLines.ts.

content.subject: P9-3: orchestrator — productLine-aware submit/decide + new error codes
content.description: server/utils/approvals.ts SubmitParams gains productLineId; iff-rule; decideRequest passes productLineId; new error codes missing_product_line and not_company_admin.

content.subject: P9-4: validators — require productLineId iff tier=productLine
content.description: SubmitApprovalSchema, AssignReviewerSchema, filtersSchema (shared/validators/).

content.subject: P9-5: API — requireCellAdmin + /api/internal/product-lines + relaxed reviewers GET
content.description: New requireCellAdmin helper. assign/unassign use it. reviewers/index.get returns canEdit, viewer, productLines. submit honours productLineId. /api/v1 contract: confirm unchanged, add explanatory comment.

content.subject: P9-6: admin UI — split ReviewerMatrix into Company + ProductLine subviews
content.description: ReviewerMatrixCompany.vue, ReviewerMatrixProductLine.vue, tabbed shell, canEdit greying, URL hash persistence.

content.subject: P9-7: publish UI — productLine picker in RequestOfficialDialog + queue badge
content.description: RequestOfficialDialog.vue gains productLine select when tier=productLine. ReviewerQueueTable.vue shows third badge.

content.subject: P9-8: listing UI — inline ProductLinePill on filter rail + ExtHero label
content.description: New ProductLinePill.vue, FilterBar plumbing, search/query.ts predicate, ExtHero.vue renders "Wireless Official" style label.

content.subject: P9-9: tests — repository, orchestrator, component, e2e
content.description: Extend reviewers + approvals integration tests; new orchestrator tests; new ReviewerMatrixProductLine unit; extend approval.spec e2e (round-trip + delegation + negative).

content.subject: P9-10: docs — ADR addendum, CLAUDE.md #12, docs/plan.md, features.md
content.description: ADR-0001 2026-06-08 addendum; CLAUDE.md decision #12 rewrite; docs/plan.md §3 + §13; docs/features.md one-liner.

content.subject: P9-1: schema + migration + seed for product_lines and product_line_id
content.description: Drizzle schema additions (product_lines table; product_line_id on approval_reviewers, approval_requests, extensions; CHECK constraints; partial unique indexes). Generate migration 0009; add inline seed for the 4 product lines. Update db seed script if needed.

content.subject: P9-2: repo layer — thread productLineId through reviewers + approvals + new productLines repo
content.description: server/repositories/reviewers.ts (listMatrix, listCellsForUser, findReviewersFor, insertReviewer, isCompanyAdminForSubCat). server/repositories/approvals.ts (ApprovalRequestRow, listPendingForCells, isReviewerForCell, setExtensionOfficialTier). New server/repositories/productLines.ts.

content.subject: P9-3: orchestrator — productLine-aware submit/decide + new error codes
content.description: server/utils/approvals.ts SubmitParams gains productLineId; iff-rule; decideRequest passes productLineId; new error codes missing_product_line and not_company_admin.

content.subject: P9-4: validators — require productLineId iff tier=productLine
content.description: SubmitApprovalSchema, AssignReviewerSchema, filtersSchema (shared/validators/).

content.subject: P9-5: API — requireCellAdmin + /api/internal/product-lines + relaxed reviewers GET
content.description: New requireCellAdmin helper. assign/unassign use it. reviewers/index.get returns canEdit, viewer, productLines. submit honours productLineId. /api/v1 contract: confirm unchanged, add explanatory comment.

content.subject: P9-6: admin UI — split ReviewerMatrix into Company + ProductLine subviews
content.description: ReviewerMatrixCompany.vue, ReviewerMatrixProductLine.vue, tabbed shell, canEdit greying, URL hash persistence.

content.subject: P9-7: publish UI — productLine picker in RequestOfficialDialog + queue badge
content.description: RequestOfficialDialog.vue gains productLine select when tier=productLine. ReviewerQueueTable.vue shows third badge.

content.subject: P9-8: listing UI — inline ProductLinePill on filter rail + ExtHero label
content.description: New ProductLinePill.vue, FilterBar plumbing, search/query.ts predicate, ExtHero.vue renders "Wireless Official" style label.

content.subject: P9-9: tests — repository, orchestrator, component, e2e
content.description: Extend reviewers + approvals integration tests; new orchestrator tests; new ReviewerMatrixProductLine unit; extend approval.spec e2e (round-trip + delegation + negative).

content.subject: P9-10: docs — ADR addendum, CLAUDE.md #12, docs/plan.md, features.md
content.description: ADR-0001 2026-06-08 addendum; CLAUDE.md decision #12 rewrite; docs/plan.md §3 + §13; docs/features.md one-liner.

content.subject: P9-1: schema + migration + seed for product_lines and product_line_id
content.description: Drizzle schema additions (product_lines table; product_line_id on approval_reviewers, approval_requests, extensions; CHECK constraints; partial unique indexes). Generate migration 0009; add inline seed for the 4 product lines. Update db seed script if needed.

content.subject: P9-2: repo layer — thread productLineId through reviewers + approvals + new productLines repo
content.description: server/repositories/reviewers.ts (listMatrix, listCellsForUser, findReviewersFor, insertReviewer, isCompanyAdminForSubCat). server/repositories/approvals.ts (ApprovalRequestRow, listPendingForCells, isReviewerForCell, setExtensionOfficialTier). New server/repositories/productLines.ts.

content.subject: P9-3: orchestrator — productLine-aware submit/decide + new error codes
content.description: server/utils/approvals.ts SubmitParams gains productLineId; iff-rule; decideRequest passes productLineId; new error codes missing_product_line and not_company_admin.

content.subject: P9-4: validators — require productLineId iff tier=productLine
content.description: SubmitApprovalSchema, AssignReviewerSchema, filtersSchema (shared/validators/).

content.subject: P9-5: API — requireCellAdmin + /api/internal/product-lines + relaxed reviewers GET
content.description: New requireCellAdmin helper. assign/unassign use it. reviewers/index.get returns canEdit, viewer, productLines. submit honours productLineId. /api/v1 contract: confirm unchanged, add explanatory comment.

content.subject: P9-6: admin UI — split ReviewerMatrix into Company + ProductLine subviews
content.description: ReviewerMatrixCompany.vue, ReviewerMatrixProductLine.vue, tabbed shell, canEdit greying, URL hash persistence.

content.subject: P9-7: publish UI — productLine picker in RequestOfficialDialog + queue badge
content.description: RequestOfficialDialog.vue gains productLine select when tier=productLine. ReviewerQueueTable.vue shows third badge.

content.subject: P9-8: listing UI — inline ProductLinePill on filter rail + ExtHero label
content.description: New ProductLinePill.vue, FilterBar plumbing, search/query.ts predicate, ExtHero.vue renders "Wireless Official" style label.

content.subject: P9-9: tests — repository, orchestrator, component, e2e
content.description: Extend reviewers + approvals integration tests; new orchestrator tests; new ReviewerMatrixProductLine unit; extend approval.spec e2e (round-trip + delegation + negative).

content.subject: P9-10: docs — ADR addendum, CLAUDE.md #12, docs/plan.md, features.md
content.description: ADR-0001 2026-06-08 addendum; CLAUDE.md decision #12 rewrite; docs/plan.md §3 + §13; docs/features.md one-liner.

beforeEach(async () => {
    await db.delete(approvalRequests)
  })

  it("returns the user's requests with extension display fields joined in", async () => {
    await db.insert(approvalRequests).values({
      id: "req-1",
      extensionId: "ext-a",
      requestedTier: "company",
      subCat: "softDev",
      requestedByUserId: "u-pub",
      reason: "Lots of usage.",
      status: "pending",
    })
    const rows = await getRequestsForUser("u-pub")
    expect(rows).toHaveLength(1)
    expect(rows[0]).toMatchObject({
      requestId: "req-1",
      extensionId: "ext-a",
      slug: "ext-a",
      name: "Ext A",
      category: "skills",
      iconColor: "#abc",
      requestedTier: "company",
      subCat: "softDev",
      status: "pending",
      reason: "Lots of usage.",
      reviewerNote: null,
      decidedAt: null,
    })
    expect(rows[0]!.createdAt).toBeInstanceOf(Date)
  })

  it("orders by createdAt desc — most recent submission first", async () => {
    await db.insert(approvalRequests).values({
      id: "req-old",
      extensionId: "ext-a",
      requestedTier: "productLine",
      subCat: "softDev",
      requestedByUserId: "u-pub",
      status: "pending",
    })
    await db
      .update(approvalRequests)
      .set({ createdAt: new Date("2026-06-01T10:00:00Z") })
      .where(approvalsIdEq("req-old"))

    await db.insert(approvalRequests).values({
      id: "req-new",
      extensionId: "ext-b",
      requestedTier: "company",
      subCat: "docs",
      requestedByUserId: "u-pub",
      status: "pending",
    })
    await db
      .update(approvalRequests)
      .set({ createdAt: new Date("2026-06-05T10:00:00Z") })
      .where(approvalsIdEq("req-new"))

    const rows = await getRequestsForUser("u-pub")
    expect(rows.map((r) => r.requestId)).toEqual(["req-new", "req-old"])
  })

  it("does not leak another user's requests", async () => {
    await db.insert(approvalRequests).values({
      id: "req-other",
      extensionId: "ext-a",
      requestedTier: "company",
      subCat: "softDev",
      // Same extension, different requester.
      requestedByUserId: "u-other",
      status: "pending",
    })

content.subject: P9-1: schema + migration + seed for product_lines and product_line_id
content.description: Drizzle schema additions (product_lines table; product_line_id on approval_reviewers, approval_requests, extensions; CHECK constraints; partial unique indexes). Generate migration 0009; add inline seed for the 4 product lines. Update db seed script if needed.

content.subject: P9-2: repo layer — thread productLineId through reviewers + approvals + new productLines repo
content.description: server/repositories/reviewers.ts (listMatrix, listCellsForUser, findReviewersFor, insertReviewer, isCompanyAdminForSubCat). server/repositories/approvals.ts (ApprovalRequestRow, listPendingForCells, isReviewerForCell, setExtensionOfficialTier). New server/repositories/productLines.ts.

content.subject: P9-3: orchestrator — productLine-aware submit/decide + new error codes
content.description: server/utils/approvals.ts SubmitParams gains productLineId; iff-rule; decideRequest passes productLineId; new error codes missing_product_line and not_company_admin.

content.subject: P9-4: validators — require productLineId iff tier=productLine
content.description: SubmitApprovalSchema, AssignReviewerSchema, filtersSchema (shared/validators/).

content.subject: P9-5: API — requireCellAdmin + /api/internal/product-lines + relaxed reviewers GET
content.description: New requireCellAdmin helper. assign/unassign use it. reviewers/index.get returns canEdit, viewer, productLines. submit honours productLineId. /api/v1 contract: confirm unchanged, add explanatory comment.

content.subject: P9-6: admin UI — split ReviewerMatrix into Company + ProductLine subviews
content.description: ReviewerMatrixCompany.vue, ReviewerMatrixProductLine.vue, tabbed shell, canEdit greying, URL hash persistence.

content.subject: P9-7: publish UI — productLine picker in RequestOfficialDialog + queue badge
content.description: RequestOfficialDialog.vue gains productLine select when tier=productLine. ReviewerQueueTable.vue shows third badge.

content.subject: P9-8: listing UI — inline ProductLinePill on filter rail + ExtHero label
content.description: New ProductLinePill.vue, FilterBar plumbing, search/query.ts predicate, ExtHero.vue renders "Wireless Official" style label.

content.subject: P9-9: tests — repository, orchestrator, component, e2e
content.description: Extend reviewers + approvals integration tests; new orchestrator tests; new ReviewerMatrixProductLine unit; extend approval.spec e2e (round-trip + delegation + negative).

content.subject: P9-10: docs — ADR addendum, CLAUDE.md #12, docs/plan.md, features.md
content.description: ADR-0001 2026-06-08 addendum; CLAUDE.md decision #12 rewrite; docs/plan.md §3 + §13; docs/features.md one-liner.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(p9-repo): thread productLineId through approvals repo, orchestrator, validators

Repos
- approvalReviewers + approvalRequests gain productLineId on every row,
  cell, signature, and select projection. New isCompanyAdminForSubCat /
  listCompanySubCatsForUser helpers back the delegated matrix-edit gate;
  findReviewerById is new for the load-then-authorise unassign path.
- setExtensionOfficialTier writes both officialTier and productLineId
  atomically so the DB CHECK never sees a half-stamped row.
- New server/repositories/productLines.ts wraps the seeded lookup with a
  list + existence read; size is small enough to cache per request.

Orchestrator
- SubmitParams + the underlying insert carry productLineId. New error
  codes missing_product_line / unexpected_product_line surface the
  iff-rule before the DB CHECK ever fires, with 400 mappings on the
  submit endpoint. decideRequest threads the cell's productLineId into
  the reviewer authorisation check and the extension-tier stamp so a
  productLine reviewer in 'wireless' cannot ride along on a 'datacom'
  request.
- listReviewerQueue's super-admin fan-out keys its dedup on
  productLineId so company-cells and productLine-cells never collapse
  into one another.

Validators
- SubmitApprovalSchema + AssignReviewerSchema add a ProductLineId field
  with a kebab-case shape check and a refine that enforces the iff-rule
  against the tier field.
- filtersSchema picks up an optional productLineId so the listing-page
  rail can round-trip ?productLineId=wireless on its own.

Tests
- Orchestrator unit + repository integration suites updated for the new
  signatures and gain coverage for the productLine-distinct-cells path
  and the tier-leak guards.
EOF
)"
