1	import { betterAuth } from "better-auth"
2	import { drizzleAdapter } from "better-auth/adapters/drizzle"
3	import { createError, getRequestHeaders, type H3Event } from "h3"
4	
5	import { isSuperAdmin } from "~~/server/repositories/reviewers"
6	import {
7	  accounts,
8	  sessions,
9	  users,
10	  verifications,
11	} from "~~/shared/db/schema/auth"
12	
13	import { useDb } from "./db"
14	
15	function makeAuth() {
16	  const db = useDb()
17	  return betterAuth({
18	    database: drizzleAdapter(db, {
19	      provider: "pg",
20	      schema: {
21	        user: users,
22	        session: sessions,
23	        account: accounts,
24	        verification: verifications,
25	      },
26	    }),
27	    emailAndPassword: { enabled: true },
28	    user: {
29	      additionalFields: {
30	        locale: {
31	          type: "string",
32	          required: false,
33	          defaultValue: "en",
34	          input: false,
35	        },
36	        themePreference: {
37	          type: "string",
38	          required: false,
39	          defaultValue: "ivory",
40	          input: false,
41	        },
42	        defaultDeptId: {
43	          type: "string",
44	          required: false,
45	          input: false,
46	        },
47	      },
48	    },
49	  })
50	}
51	
52	type AuthInstance = ReturnType<typeof makeAuth>
53	
54	let _auth: AuthInstance | undefined
55	
56	export function useAuthServer(): AuthInstance {
57	  if (!_auth) _auth = makeAuth()
58	  return _auth
59	}
60	
61	type SessionPayload = Awaited<
62	  ReturnType<AuthInstance["api"]["getSession"]>
63	>
64	export type SessionUser = NonNullable<SessionPayload>["user"]
65	
66	function toFetchHeaders(raw: Record<string, string | string[] | undefined>): Headers {
67	  const headers = new Headers()
68	  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(raw)) {
69	    if (value === undefined) continue
70	    headers.append(key, Array.isArray(value) ? value.join(", ") : value)
71	  }
72	  return headers
73	}
74	
75	export async function getSessionUser(event: H3Event): Promise<SessionUser | null> {
76	  const auth = useAuthServer()
77	  const session = await auth.api.getSession({
78	    headers: toFetchHeaders(getRequestHeaders(event)),
79	  })
80	  return session?.user ?? null
81	}
82	
83	export async function requireUser(event: H3Event): Promise<SessionUser> {
84	  const user = await getSessionUser(event)
85	  if (!user) {
86	    throw createError({ statusCode: 401, statusMessage: "Unauthenticated" })
87	  }
88	  return user
89	}
90	
91	// Super-admin gate for the reviewer-matrix admin surface. Returns the
92	// session user when they hold any membership with role='superAdmin';
93	// throws 401 when unauthenticated and 403 otherwise. The membership
94	// lookup goes through the reviewers repo so the policy lives in one
95	// place — see `server/repositories/reviewers.ts`.
96	export async function requireSuperAdmin(event: H3Event): Promise<SessionUser> {
97	  const user = await requireUser(event)
98	  if (!(await isSuperAdmin(useDb(), user.id))) {
99	    throw createError({ statusCode: 403, statusMessage: "Forbidden" })
100	  }
101	  return user
102	}
103

lines: +// Cell-aware authorisation for matrix edits. Encodes the delegation rule
+//   - company-tier admins of subCat X may manage productLine cells of
+// Callers pass the cell coordinates explicitly — for `unassign`, load the

toolUseResult.originalFile:
import { and, desc, eq, ilike, inArray, isNotNull, isNull, like, or, sql, type SQL, type ExtractTablesWithRelations } from "drizzle-orm"
import type { PgDatabase, PgQueryResultHKT } from "drizzle-orm/pg-core"

import { MY_DEPT_ID } from "~~/shared/data/departments"
import type * as schema from "~~/shared/db/schema"
import { extensions, extensionTags } from "~~/shared/db/schema"
import type { Filters } from "~~/shared/validators/filters"

const ALL_DEPTS = "__all"

// Driver-agnostic: matches postgres-js, PGlite, and PgTransaction so the
// query builder composes with the repository layer's `Transactable`.
type Db = PgDatabase<
  PgQueryResultHKT,
  typeof schema,
  ExtractTablesWithRelations<typeof schema>
>

export function buildExtensionWhere(
  db: Db,
  filters: Filters,
  fallbackDeptId?: string,
): SQL | undefined {
  const clauses = [
    eq(extensions.visibility, "published"),

    filters.category ? eq(extensions.category, filters.category) : undefined,
    filters.scope ? eq(extensions.scope, filters.scope) : undefined,
    filters.funcCat ? eq(extensions.funcCat, filters.funcCat) : undefined,
    filters.subCat ? eq(extensions.subCat, filters.subCat) : undefined,
    filters.l2 ? eq(extensions.l2, filters.l2) : undefined,

    filters.creator
      ? eq(extensions.publisherUserId, filters.creator)
      : undefined,
    filters.publisher
      ? eq(extensions.ownerOrgId, filters.publisher)
      : undefined,

    (() => {
      const dept = filters.dept ?? fallbackDeptId ?? MY_DEPT_ID
      if (dept === ALL_DEPTS) return undefined
      // NULL deptId means "system-scoped, applies to every dept" — the
      // catalog seeder and seed-mcp-landscape both leave it null because
      // their entries are cross-org. Without this branch, those rows are
      // invisible to anyone whose default filter lands on a real dept.
      return or(
        eq(extensions.deptId, dept),
        like(extensions.deptId, `${dept}.%`),
        sql`${extensions.deptId} IS NULL`,
      )
    })(),

    filters.filter === "trending"
      ? sql`${extensions.downloadsCount} > 50000`
      : undefined,
    filters.filter === "new" ? eq(extensions.badge, "new") : undefined,
    // Legacy "official" chip = any official tier (productLine OR company).
    // The new finer-grained `tier` filter clause runs alongside this one.
    filters.filter === "official"
      ? isNotNull(extensions.officialTier)
      : undefined,
    filters.filter === "free"
      ? sql`${extensions.licenseSpdx} IS NOT NULL`
      : undefined,

    filters.tier === "unofficial" ? isNull(extensions.officialTier) : undefined,
    filters.tier === "productLine"
      ? eq(extensions.officialTier, "productLine")
      : undefined,
    filters.tier === "company"
      ? eq(extensions.officialTier, "company")
      : undefined,

    filters.q
      ? or(
          sql`${extensions.searchVector} @@ websearch_to_tsquery('simple', ${filters.q})`,
          ilike(extensions.name, `%${filters.q}%`),
          ilike(extensions.nameZh, `%${filters.q}%`),
        )
      : undefined,

    filters.tags && filters.tags.length > 0
      ? inArray(
          extensions.id,
          db
            .select({ id: extensionTags.extensionId })
            .from(extensionTags)
            .where(inArray(extensionTags.tagId, filters.tags))
            .groupBy(extensionTags.extensionId)
            .having(
              filters.tagMatch === "all"
                ? sql`count(*) = ${filters.tags.length}`
                : sql`count(*) >= 1`,
            ),
        )
      : undefined,
  ].filter((c): c is SQL => Boolean(c))

  return clauses.length > 0 ? and(...clauses) : undefined
}

export function buildExtensionOrder(sort: Filters["sort"]) {
  switch (sort) {
    case "stars":
      return [desc(extensions.starsAvg), desc(extensions.downloadsCount)]
    case "recent":
      return [desc(extensions.publishedAt), desc(extensions.createdAt)]
    case "downloads":
    default:
      return [desc(extensions.downloadsCount)]
  }
}

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(p9-api): cell-aware permission gates and /api/internal/product-lines

- New requireCellAdmin(event, cell) helper in server/utils/auth.ts encodes
  the delegation rule: super-admins anywhere, company admins of subCat X
  may manage productLine cells of subCat X (any productLine), and company-
  tier cells remain super-admin-only.
- assign.post.ts gates on requireCellAdmin against the request body.
  unassign.delete.ts loads the target row first and gates on the row's
  own coordinates so the id alone cannot escalate.
- reviewers GET widens to "super-admin OR any company-tier reviewer",
  joins productLines into the response, and annotates each row with
  canEdit so the admin UI can grey non-editable cells in one round-trip.
- New /api/internal/product-lines lists the seeded product lines for the
  publish dialog, the admin matrix, and the listing filter rail.
- /api/v1 contract is unchanged; comment near the badge mapper notes that
  productLineId is intentionally suppressed.
- shared/search/query.ts honours filters.productLineId so the listing
  page can compose ?tier=productLine&productLineId=wireless.
EOF
)"

content.subject: P9-1: schema + migration + seed for product_lines and product_line_id
content.description: Drizzle schema additions (product_lines table; product_line_id on approval_reviewers, approval_requests, extensions; CHECK constraints; partial unique indexes). Generate migration 0009; add inline seed for the 4 product lines. Update db seed script if needed.

content.subject: P9-2: repo layer — thread productLineId through reviewers + approvals + new productLines repo
content.description: server/repositories/reviewers.ts (listMatrix, listCellsForUser, findReviewersFor, insertReviewer, isCompanyAdminForSubCat). server/repositories/approvals.ts (ApprovalRequestRow, listPendingForCells, isReviewerForCell, setExtensionOfficialTier). New server/repositories/productLines.ts.

content.subject: P9-3: orchestrator — productLine-aware submit/decide + new error codes
content.description: server/utils/approvals.ts SubmitParams gains productLineId; iff-rule; decideRequest passes productLineId; new error codes missing_product_line and not_company_admin.

content.subject: P9-4: validators — require productLineId iff tier=productLine
content.description: SubmitApprovalSchema, AssignReviewerSchema, filtersSchema (shared/validators/).

content.subject: P9-5: API — requireCellAdmin + /api/internal/product-lines + relaxed reviewers GET
content.description: New requireCellAdmin helper. assign/unassign use it. reviewers/index.get returns canEdit, viewer, productLines. submit honours productLineId. /api/v1 contract: confirm unchanged, add explanatory comment.

content.subject: P9-6: admin UI — split ReviewerMatrix into Company + ProductLine subviews
content.description: ReviewerMatrixCompany.vue, ReviewerMatrixProductLine.vue, tabbed shell, canEdit greying, URL hash persistence.

content.subject: P9-7: publish UI — productLine picker in RequestOfficialDialog + queue badge
content.description: RequestOfficialDialog.vue gains productLine select when tier=productLine. ReviewerQueueTable.vue shows third badge.

content.subject: P9-8: listing UI — inline ProductLinePill on filter rail + ExtHero label
content.description: New ProductLinePill.vue, FilterBar plumbing, search/query.ts predicate, ExtHero.vue renders "Wireless Official" style label.

content.subject: P9-9: tests — repository, orchestrator, component, e2e
content.description: Extend reviewers + approvals integration tests; new orchestrator tests; new ReviewerMatrixProductLine unit; extend approval.spec e2e (round-trip + delegation + negative).

content.subject: P9-10: docs — ADR addendum, CLAUDE.md #12, docs/plan.md, features.md
content.description: ADR-0001 2026-06-08 addendum; CLAUDE.md decision #12 rewrite; docs/plan.md §3 + §13; docs/features.md one-liner.

lines: +// Tabbed shell over the two tier-specific matrix views.
+// Product-Line tab — (subCat × productLine) grid. Edits permitted for
+//   super-admins anywhere and for company admins of the row's subCat
+// The active tab persists in the URL hash so deep links survive a reload.
+// Provide a Ref<boolean> for the subviews so they don't need to thread
+// the viewer prop themselves. Wrapped as a computed so reactivity follows
+// the prop without an explicit re-broadcast.

content.content:
1	// @vitest-environment nuxt
2	import { afterEach, beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from "vitest"
3	import { mountSuspended } from "@nuxt/test-utils/runtime"
4	import { defineComponent, h, nextTick } from "vue"
5	
6	import ReviewerMatrix from "./ReviewerMatrix.vue"
7	
8	const fetchMock = vi.fn()
9	const realFetch = (globalThis as { $fetch?: unknown }).$fetch
10	
11	beforeEach(() => {
12	  fetchMock.mockReset()
13	  ;(globalThis as unknown as { $fetch: typeof fetchMock }).$fetch = fetchMock
14	})
15	
16	afterEach(() => {
17	  ;(globalThis as unknown as { $fetch: unknown }).$fetch = realFetch
18	})
19	
20	// Same passthrough strategy as RequestOfficialDialog.nuxt.test.ts — the
21	// matrix mounts an add-reviewer dialog that wouldn't otherwise render in
22	// the headless env.
23	const passthrough = (name: string) =>
24	  defineComponent({ name, render() { return h("div", this.$slots.default?.()) } })
25	
26	const stubs = {
27	  Dialog: passthrough("Dialog"),
28	  DialogContent: passthrough("DialogContent"),
29	  DialogTitle: passthrough("DialogTitle"),
30	  DialogDescription: passthrough("DialogDescription"),
31	  Label: defineComponent({
32	    name: "LabelStub",
33	    props: { for: { type: String, required: false } },
34	    render() { return h("label", { for: this.for }, this.$slots.default?.()) },
35	  }),
36	  Input: defineComponent({
37	    name: "InputStub",
38	    props: {
39	      modelValue: { type: String, default: "" },
40	      type: { type: String, default: "text" },
41	    },
42	    emits: ["update:modelValue"],
43	    render() {
44	      return h("input", {
45	        type: this.type,
46	        value: this.modelValue,
47	        onInput: (e: Event) =>
48	          this.$emit("update:modelValue", (e.target as HTMLInputElement).value),
49	      })
50	    },
51	  }),
52	}
53	
54	const REVIEWERS = [
55	  {
56	    id: "rev-1",
57	    tier: "productLine" as const,
58	    subCat: "softDev",
59	    userId: "u-ben",
60	    userEmail: "ben.example.test",
61	    userName: "Ben Park",
62	  },
63	  {
64	    id: "rev-2",
65	    tier: "company" as const,
66	    subCat: "softDev",
67	    userId: "u-amy",
68	    userEmail: "amy.example.test",
69	    userName: null,
70	  },
71	]
72	
73	describe("ReviewerMatrix", () => {
74	  it("renders one row per FUNC_TAXONOMY l1 leaf (9 rows) and two tier columns", async () => {
75	    const wrapper = await mountSuspended(ReviewerMatrix, {
76	      props: { reviewers: [] },
77	      global: { stubs },
78	    })
79	    // 9 leaves: systemDesign, softDev, testing, network, embedded, cloud,
80	    // docs, data, vcs.
81	    const bodyRows = wrapper.findAll("tbody tr")
82	    expect(bodyRows).toHaveLength(9)
83	    const headers = wrapper.findAll("thead th")
84	    // Subcat column + 2 tier columns = 3 thead cells.
85	    expect(headers).toHaveLength(3)
86	    // The two tier headers come from the i18n keys.
87	    expect(headers[1]!.text()).toContain("Product-Line Official")
88	    expect(headers[2]!.text()).toContain("Company Official")
89	  })
90	
91	  it("renders an existing reviewer chip with their name when name is set, email when not", async () => {
92	    const wrapper = await mountSuspended(ReviewerMatrix, {
93	      props: { reviewers: REVIEWERS },
94	      global: { stubs },
95	    })
96	    const text = wrapper.text()
97	    expect(text).toContain("Ben Park")
98	    // amy has userName: null, so the chip falls back to email.
99	    expect(text).toContain("amy.example.test")
100	  })
101	
102	  it("clicking + Add opens the dialog with the cell's tier and subCat in the subtitle", async () => {
103	    const wrapper = await mountSuspended(ReviewerMatrix, {
104	      props: { reviewers: [] },
105	      global: { stubs },
106	    })
107	    // Each empty cell shows a "+ Add" button. Click the first one (which
108	    // corresponds to productLine × systemDesign).
109	    const addBtns = wrapper.findAll("button").filter((b) => b.text().includes("Add"))
110	    expect(addBtns.length).toBeGreaterThan(0)
111	    await addBtns[0]!.trigger("click")
112	    await nextTick()
113	    // The dialog opens — input becomes visible.
114	    expect(wrapper.find('input[type="email"]').exists()).toBe(true)
115	  })
116	
117	  it("lookup returning null user surfaces the noSuchUser hint", async () => {
118	    fetchMock.mockResolvedValueOnce({ user: null })
119	    const wrapper = await mountSuspended(ReviewerMatrix, {
120	      props: { reviewers: [] },
121	      global: { stubs },
122	    })
123	    await wrapper
124	      .findAll("button")
125	      .find((b) => b.text().includes("Add"))!
126	      .trigger("click")
127	    await nextTick()
128	    await wrapper.find('input[type="email"]').setValue("ghost.example.test")
129	    await wrapper
130	      .findAll("button")
131	      .find((b) => b.text() === "Add reviewer")!
132	      .trigger("click")
133	    await nextTick()
134	    expect(wrapper.text()).toContain("No user with that email")
135	    // Only one $fetch call — the by-email lookup. /assign never fires.
136	    expect(fetchMock).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)
137	  })
138	
139	  it("lookup returning a user posts /assign with the cell's (tier, subCat, userId) and emits refresh", async () => {
140	    fetchMock
141	      .mockResolvedValueOnce({ user: { id: "u-cory", email: "cory.example.test", name: "Cory" } })
142	      .mockResolvedValueOnce({ ok: true })
143	    const wrapper = await mountSuspended(ReviewerMatrix, {
144	      props: { reviewers: [] },
145	      global: { stubs },
146	    })
147	    // Click the first + Add (productLine × systemDesign).
148	    await wrapper
149	      .findAll("button")
150	      .find((b) => b.text().includes("Add"))!
151	      .trigger("click")
152	    await nextTick()
153	    await wrapper.find('input[type="email"]').setValue("cory.example.test")
154	    await wrapper
155	      .findAll("button")
156	      .find((b) => b.text() === "Add reviewer")!
157	      .trigger("click")
158	    await nextTick()
159	
160	    expect(fetchMock).toHaveBeenNthCalledWith(
161	      1,
162	      "/api/internal/admin/users/by-email",
163	      expect.objectContaining({ query: { email: "cory.example.test" } }),
164	    )
165	    expect(fetchMock).toHaveBeenNthCalledWith(
166	      2,
167	      "/api/internal/admin/reviewers/assign",
168	      expect.objectContaining({
169	        method: "POST",
170	        body: { tier: "productLine", subCat: "systemDesign", userId: "u-cory" },
171	      }),
172	    )
173	    expect(wrapper.emitted("refresh")).toHaveLength(1)
174	  })
175	
176	  it("clicking the X on a chip posts /unassign with the chip's id and emits refresh", async () => {
177	    fetchMock.mockResolvedValueOnce({ ok: true })
178	    const wrapper = await mountSuspended(ReviewerMatrix, {
179	      props: { reviewers: REVIEWERS },
180	      global: { stubs },
181	    })
182	    // The remove button on each chip has a localized aria-label
183	    // ("Remove ben.example.test").
184	    const removeBtn = wrapper.findAll("button").find((b) => {
185	      const aria = b.attributes("aria-label") ?? ""
186	      return aria.includes("ben.example.test")
187	    })
188	    expect(removeBtn).toBeDefined()
189	    await removeBtn!.trigger("click")
190	    await nextTick()
191	    expect(fetchMock).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
192	      "/api/internal/admin/reviewers/unassign",
193	      expect.objectContaining({
194	        method: "DELETE",
195	        body: { id: "rev-1" },
196	      }),
197	    )
198	    expect(wrapper.emitted("refresh")).toHaveLength(1)
199	  })
200	})
201

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(p9-ui-admin): split reviewer matrix by tier with per-cell canEdit

- ReviewerMatrix.vue becomes a tabbed shell over two presentational
  subcomponents. The active tab persists in the URL hash so
  /admin/reviewers#tab=productLine deep-links straight into the grid.
- ReviewerMatrixCompany.vue keeps the existing one-column layout for
  company-tier admins; ReviewerMatrixProductLine.vue is a new
  (subCat × productLine) grid that consumes the productLines list from
  the GET endpoint and disables non-editable cells in-place.
- Cells where the viewer is not authorised hide their "+ Add" tile and
  their chip "×" buttons, mirroring requireCellAdmin server-side so a
  company admin sees their corner of the matrix as actionable and the
  rest as read-only.
- /admin/reviewers page widens its middleware from require-super-admin
  to require-reviewer, and passes the new productLines + viewer payload
  through to the shell.
- Updated en/zh strings and the existing ReviewerMatrix component test
  to cover the tab switch, productLine assign payload, and the greyed-
  cell delegation case.
EOF
)"
